agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific int

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3/TM5, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 electrostatic interactions, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current design strategy is generating strong binders but insufficient select

report.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating strong binders but insufficient selectivity. The next step is to explicitly target mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions, especially in SSTR3/SSTR5, while maintaining the native pharmacophore (FWKT7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 that are likely driving off-target binding while preserving pharmacophore positions (7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
These mutations are expected to break hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions in off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets while maintaining native-like interactions in SSTR2.
